phoneticUs: 藢kr蓱藧n蓹藞l蓱藧d蕭瑟k(蓹)l
meaning: adj.鎸夊勾浠ｉ『搴忔帓鍒楃殑
examples: The facts should be presented in chronological order.
I have arranged these stories in chronological order.
My own shelves are a kind of roadmap through my life, telling a chronological tale.
The government says it will give children a complete history of Britain, in chronological order.
"It's put ... together into a cohesive, chronological line of events, " said actress Sally Field.
